review_execution.command: claude -p --model claude-opus-4-8 --effort max --permission-mode dontAsk --tools "" --output-format json < staged diff prompt > /tmp/cp00-116-claude-review-output-valid.json

findings.title: Redundant coverageKindFor mapping duplicates slugFor output
findings.evidence: coverageKindFor() hard-codes a snake_case return for each of the 10 titles, but every branch yields exactly what slugFor(title) already produces (e.g. 'Keyboard/focus behavior' -> 'keyboard_focus_behavior'), and the fallback is already `return slugFor(title)`. The if-chain is dead/duplicative.
findings.recommendation: Optional maintainability cleanup: collapse coverageKindFor to `return slugFor(title)`. No behavioral change; does not block closeout.

parsed_review.rationale: The pack is a metadata-only, synthetic-only Risk A binding pack and stays inside scope. CP115 UI readiness inheritance is real: it imports PERMISSION_KERNEL_CP115_API_FIXTURE_UI_READINESS_CONTRACT and createPermissionKernelCp115ApiFixtureUiReadinessMatrix and threads inherited counts/contract_id through the surface, manifest, and contract (inherited_ui_readiness_contract_id). All required security boundaries hold: permission_badge.grants_access is hard-coded false; secondary_interaction.enabled is false when effect==='deny' and requires_review is true for 'review_required' while grants_access/grants_human_approval/executes_workflow stay false; audit_hint_display is preview_only:true with emitted_to_audit_ledger:false. Hidden-source-field leakage is prevented by a whitelist sanitizeHint() that rebuilds the hint from only six allowed keys (it does not spread the caller object), so the four CP113-inherited hidden_source_fields (privileged_note, cross_tenant_secret, internal_policy_label, sealed_audit_hint_payload) are dropped — verified both by the validator and tests that inject those fields and assert undefined. Unauthorized-count leakage is avoided: redacted_unauthorized_count copy is the count-free string 'Some unavailable items are hidden.' and exposes_unauthorized_count/exposes_hidden_field_names are false. freezeSurface() pins all no-write/no-leak flags after the spread so request input cannot override them; the no_write_attestation (15 flags incl. mutates_permission_policy, writes_audit_event, writes_product_state, creates_database_rows, persists_idempotency_keys, acquires_locks, executes_export_download, executes_external_share, executes_ai_retrieval, grants_human_approval, executes_claude_review, implements_ldip) is all-false in the contract JSON, the JS contract, and per-surface, and is asserted false by both the validator and tests. ui_runtime_route_added/runtime_ui_route_added/bound_to_runtime_route are false — no runtime UI or API route is introduced (factories are pure, no fetch/fs/db/I/O). HRX stays embedded-only (hrx_embedded_boundary='embedded_people_hr_evidence_module_inside_law_firm_os'). Handoff is fixed to CP00-117 / RP02.P04.M05.S18. Coverage math is consistent across contract, code, tests, and validator: 10 units S08-S17, deliverable counts ui:3/security_audit:2/implementation:3/fixture:1/test:1, 3-surface matrix (allow/deny/review). All 11 public_exports listed in the contract JSON exist in the module and are imported by the validator; binding_surfaces, permission_badge_shape, audit_hint_shape, error_copy_codes, and hidden_source_fields are cross-checked. Claude packet is read_only:true, effort:max, exactly_one_valid_pack_review_required:true — consistent with this single pack-level review. Build-smoke and Hermes command anchors are inert strings, not executed. Only a single trivial P3 maintainability nit found; nothing affects security, scope, or closeout readiness. Note: the validator's requireFalse/requireIncludes helpers are referenced but defined outside this diff; they are consistent with the established CP108-CP115 validation pattern and not introduced here.
